rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing, gap, and thesis claims are appropriately constructed.
rationale.R1.2: The request entails checking whether the argument from method intuition to metric gains to conclusions is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the section makes explicit claims from reported benchmark numbers, review centrally includes checking evidence-claim alignment.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact asserts a new state of the art and advancement over prior work, making novelty positioning a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be determined from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding endorsement of unsupported state-of-the-art or essentiality claims.
rationale.RX.3: The task is limited to reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or expanding the paper.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid downstream harm from overstated scientific claims or misleading benchmark interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to statements and claims present in the artifact.
